steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined sentence in the passage. The sentence describes Yung Wing writing about an imaginary trip to New York as a child — and the very next sentence states a general lesson: imagination sometimes foreshadows what is most on our minds.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the narrator confuses fantasy with memory': The passage never suggests Yung is confused about what was real. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Yung published a childhood story': Nothing in the passage mentions publishing. ✗
  • Choice C — 'an event that exemplifies a phenomenon': The childhood writing episode is the specific example that illustrates the broader claim made in the next sentence. ✓
  • Choice D — 'evidence of Yung's general adventurousness': The passage draws no such theme; the sentence serves a structural, not characterizing, role. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
